### 1. Calculations for the Golden Rectangle

Given:
- The width of the golden rectangle = 2 meters
- The golden ratio φ = (1 + √5) / 2 ≈ 1.618

#### a. Length of the Rectangle

Using the golden ratio formula:
$$
\text{length} = \text{width} \times \phi
$$
$$
\text{length} = 2 \times \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} = 1 + \sqrt{5} \text{ meters}
$$

#### b. Area of the Rectangle

$$
\text{Area} = \text{length} \times \text{width}
$$
$$
\text{Area} = (1 + \sqrt{5}) \times 2 = 2(1 + \sqrt{5}) \text{ square meters}
$$

#### c. Length of the Diagonal

Using the Pythagorean theorem:
$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{\text{length}^2 + \text{width}^2}
$$
$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{(1 + \sqrt{5})^2 + 2^2}
$$
$$
(1 + \sqrt{5})^2 = 1 + 2\sqrt{5} + 5 = 6 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$
$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{6 + 2\sqrt{5} + 4} = \sqrt{10 + 2\sqrt{5}} \text{ meters}
$$

---

### 2. Calculations for the Inscribed Circle

#### a. Radius of the Inscribed Circle

Since the circle is inscribed within the rectangle, its diameter equals the width of the rectangle, which is 2 meters. Therefore:
$$
\text{Radius} = \frac{\text{width}}{2} = \frac{2}{2} = 1 \text{ meter}
$$

#### b. Area of the Region Inside the Rectangle but Outside the Circle

$$
\text{Area of rectangle} = 2(1 + \sqrt{5}) \text{ square meters}
$$
$$
\text{Area of circle} = \pi \times \text{radius}^2 = \pi \times 1^2
